title: Machine Learning for historical bowed instruments
shortDescription: Research project conducted with UCLouvain and the MIM to identify reduced historical violins using machine learning.
description: Master's thesis conducted in collaboration with UCLouvain and the Musical Instruments Museum (MIM) in Brussels. The project investigated the automatic identification of reduced versus non-reduced historical bowed instruments from high-precision 3D photogrammetry data. I designed, implemented, and evaluated Multiple Kernel Learning (EasyMKL), Convolutional Neural Networks, transfer learning architectures, and Multi-Layer Perceptrons on a highly imbalanced dataset of historical violins. The work included feature engineering from contour-line geometry, image-based processing of elevation maps, nested cross-validation, hyperparameter optimization, data augmentation, ablation studies, and model interpretability analysis.
